monday.objective: Students will solve multi-step equations with variables on both sides

anticipatory_set.original_content: Warm-up: Review solving one-step and two-step equations. Quick quiz on whiteboard.

instructional_input.original_content: Direct instruction on solving equations with variables on both sides. Model 3-4 examples on board, explaining each step. Emphasize combining like terms and inverse operations.

guided_practice.original_content: Work through 5 problems together as a class. Students solve on paper, volunteers share solutions on board. Discuss common mistakes and strategies.

independent_practice.original_content: Students complete worksheet with 10 problems of varying difficulty. Circulate to provide individual support. Early finishers work on challenge problems.

assessment.original_content: Exit ticket: Solve 2 multi-step equations. Check for understanding of the process and accuracy.

homework.original_content: Complete practice problems 1-15 on page 47. Show all work.

tuesday.objective: Students will apply equation-solving skills to word problems

anticipatory_set.original_content: Review homework problems. Address common errors. Quick pair-share: When do we use equations in real life?

instructional_input.original_content: Teach strategy for translating word problems into equations. Model 3 examples: age problems, money problems, distance problems. Emphasize identifying the variable and writing the equation.

guided_practice.original_content: Small group work: Groups of 3-4 solve word problems together. Each group presents one solution to the class.

independent_practice.original_content: Individual work on word problem worksheet. 8 problems with real-world contexts. Provide sentence frames for ELL students.

assessment.original_content: Formative: Check group work and individual practice. Note students needing additional support.

homework.original_content: Create your own word problem that requires a multi-step equation. Solve it and explain your thinking.
